prompt: qui, quoi, quand, comment, pourquoi, combien — why do French question words look alike?
options: Most start with qu- — like English wh- (who, what, when)
They're all borrowed from Latin unchanged
explain: qu- is the question family. Anchor the family, then each member is one syllable of spelling.

hint: pour + quoi — literally “for what”. Two words you already know, glued.
explain: pourquoi = pour (for) + quoi (what). Never needs -s or accents.

hint: comme + -ent: sounds /kɔ.mɑ̃/ — the -ent is the nasal ‘ah’.
explain: comment — double m is the trap. comme (like) + nt.
combien = com + bien. If you can spell bien, you can spell combien.

hint: qu- + oi (the /wa/ sound, like moi, toi).
explain: quoi rhymes and spells like moi/toi — the -oi family.
